title: inDecay: deep-learning prediction of CRISPR editing indel outcomes
description:
inDecay is a deep-learning framework for predicting CRISPR editing indel outcomes by modelling indel-frequency decay, including the cross-species transfer-learning (mouse, livestock) and non-Cas9 (TREX2) analyses from the manuscript.

🔗 Latest version & ongoing development: https://github.com/StatBiomed/inDecay. This Zenodo record archives the fixed v1.0.0 snapshot for citation; for the most recent code, bug fixes and releases, please use the GitHub repository.

Repository structure

  • inDecay/ — core Python package: PATH.py (path config), reader.py & alignmap.py (sequence feature extraction), models.py / ratio_models.py (model definitions), transformation.py, analysis_fn.py & metrics.py (evaluation), evaluate.py, zygote.py.
  • scripts/ — training / fine-tuning / prediction entry points, fetch_data.py (checksummed data & weight download), and the non-Cas9 TREX2 preprocessing pipeline under scripts/noncas9_crispr/.
  • notebooks/ — figure-reproduction notebooks (Figure2, Figure3, Figures4_and_5, SHAP_K562, Supp_FrameBreakdown, Benchmarking, TREX2_QC_validation).
  • demo/ — inDecay_demo.ipynb, an end-to-end walkthrough of feature extraction and training.
  • pretrained/ — pretrained cell-line backbone checkpoints and precomputed transfer-learning predictions.
  • results/ — precomputed benchmarking / transfer tables (CSV/JSON) that the figure notebooks plot from.
  • data/ — reference scaffold (SelfTarget_NewScaffold.fasta); larger training / somatic data are pulled from figshare.
  • Top level — setup.py, requirements.txt, environment.yml, README.md, LICENSE (Apache-2.0).

Installation & reproduction

  1. conda create -n inDecay python=3.10.4 pip && conda activate inDecay
  2. pip install -r requirements.txt && pip install -e ./
  3. python scripts/fetch_data.py — pulls training/somatic data (figshare DOI 10.6084/m9.figshare.25133564) and the Figure 4 & 5 fine-tuned checkpoints (Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20977675), each verified by checksum.
  4. Run the notebooks under notebooks/ from the repository root.
